source: La Isla Network / Adelante Initiative, Ingenio San Antonio (ISA), Nicaragua; effect sizes per the 2024 ILO report on the Water-Rest-Shade (WRS) intervention.
intervention: Water-Rest-Shade (WRS): mandated breaks, shade tents, and purified water with electrolytes.

outcomes.note: Acute kidney injury (AKI) reduced ~94%; productivity increased 10-20% despite reduced functional working time, so total productivity is maintained or raised.

crew_reference.note: Illustrative reference crew for the back-test (sugarcane harvest season).

baseline_aki_note: Per-worker-season AKI incidence without intervention. Tunable; transferred from Mesoamerican agriculture to Gulf construction with uncertainty. 0.10 is a conservative default (some sugarcane cohorts reported markedly higher acute kidney-injury markers).

cost_items_usd.item: Equipment for new workers: hat, thermos, shade-tent + water-reserve share

cost_note: Illustrative figures derived from the published Adelante cost-component list; mostly small one-time capital. The economic finding is that the intervention pays for itself because productivity is maintained while injury and downtime fall.
